book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

lineage: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
A Twofold Question to Be Treated Concerning the Soul; Is It ‘Body’? and is It ‘Spirit’? What Body is.
title: A Twofold Question to Be Treated Concerning the Soul; Is It ‘Body’? and is It ‘Spirit’? What Body is.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Chapter 17 [XII.]—A Twofold Question to Be Treated Concerning the Soul; Is It “Body”? and is It “Spirit”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: And now, as far as the Lord vouchsafes to enable me, I must reply also to that allegation of yours, in which, speaking of the soul, you again mention my name, and say, “We do not, as the very able and learned bishop Augustin professes, allow it to be incorporeal and also a spirit.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: We have therefore, first, to discuss the question, whether the soul is to be deemed incorporeal, as I have said; or corporeal, as you hold.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Then, secondly, whether in our Scriptures it is called a spirit—although not the whole but its own separate part is also properly called spirit.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Well, I should, to begin with, like to know how you define body.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For if that is not “body” which does not consist of limbs of flesh, then the earth cannot be a body, nor the sky, nor a stone, nor water, nor the stars, nor anything of the kind.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: If, however, a “body” is whatever consists of parts, whether greater or less, which occupy greater or smaller local spaces, then all the things which I have just mentioned are bodies; the air is a body; the visible light is a body; and so are all the things which the apostle has in view, when he says, “There are celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial.”
